question: The Constitution (Seventy-third Amendment) Act provides for

en.content: The Constitution (Seventy-third Amendment) Act provides for

options.content: an elaborate system of establishing municipal self-government
an elaborate system of establishing panchayats as units of self-government
establishing a Commission for Linguistic Minorities
the creation of the Jharkhand State

en.explanation:
The correct choice is Option 2.

Key points of the 73rd Amendment (1992):

It inserted Part IX (Articles 243–243O) and the Eleventh Schedule into the Constitution.

It mandates a three-tier Panchayati Raj system (village, intermediate, district levels).

It provides for regular elections, reservation of seats (SC/ST and women), State Finance Commissions, and Gram Sabhas.

options: A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote
The Deputy Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha is nominated by the President from among the members of the Rajya Sabha
The Lok Sabha cannot be dissolved before the completion of its five year term
The members of Lok Sabha also vote in the election of the members of Rajya Sabha
question: Which one of the following statements about the Parliament of India is correct ?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about the Parliament of India is correct ?

options.content: A majority of members of Rajya Sabha are elected by a system of proportional representation by means of single transferable vote
The Deputy Chairperson of the Rajya Sabha is nominated by the President from among the members of the Rajya Sabha
The Lok Sabha cannot be dissolved before the completion of its five year term
The members of Lok Sabha also vote in the election of the members of Rajya Sabha

en.explanation:
The correct statement is Option 1.

• Rajya Sabha members (except the President’s nominees) are chosen by elected members of State Legislative Assemblies (and certain Union Territory legislators)

• The voting method is proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote

Why the others are wrong:

The Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha is elected by its members, not nominated by the President.

Lok Sabha can be dissolved early by the President on the Prime Minister’s advice.

Only State/UT legislators (not Lok Sabha MPs) vote for Rajya Sabha elections.

hi.content: भारत की संसद के बारे में निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही है?

options.content: राज्य सभा के सदस्यों की बहुलता का निर्वाचन आनुपातिक प्रतिनिधित्व पद्धति के अनुसार एकल संक्रमणीय मत द्वारा किया जाता है
राष्ट्रपति द्वारा राज्य सभा के सदस्यों में से राज्य सभा के उपसभापति को नामनिर्दिष्ट किया जाता है
लोक सभा को पंच वर्ष की अवधि पूरी होने से पहले विघटित नहीं किया जा सकता है
लोक सभा के सदस्य राज्य सभा के सदस्यों के चुनाव में भी मतदान करते हैं।

hi.explanation:
सही कथन विकल्प 1 है।

• राज्यसभा के सदस्य (राष्ट्रपति द्वारा मनोनीत सदस्यों को छोड़कर) राज्य विधानसभाओं (तथा कुछ संघ शासित प्रदेशों की विधानसभाओं) के निर्वाचित सदस्यों द्वारा चुने जाते हैं।

• मतदान की विधि अनुपातिक प्रतिनिधित्व (प्रोपोर्शनल रिप्रेज़ेंटेशन) होती है, जिसमें एकल संक्रमणीय मत (सिंगल ट्रांसफरेबल वोट) प्रणाली का प्रयोग किया जाता है।

अन्य विकल्प गलत क्यों हैं:

राज्यसभा के उप सभापति का निर्वाचन उसके सदस्यों द्वारा किया जाता है, वे राष्ट्रपति द्वारा नामांकित नहीं होते।

लोकसभा को राष्ट्रपति प्रधानमंत्री की सलाह पर समय से पूर्व भंग कर सकते हैं।

राज्य/संघ शासित प्रदेशों के विधायक ही राज्यसभा के चुनाव में मतदान करते हैं, लोकसभा के सांसद मतदान नहीं करते।

The actual Five Principles of Panchsheel are:

Mutual non-aggression

Mutual non-interference in each other’s internal affairs

Equality and mutual benefit

question:
Consider the following features :
1.Consent of the governed
2.Political equality
3.Accountability of the ruled to the ruler

Which of the above feature/features outline democratic rule?

en.content:
Consider the following features :
1.Consent of the governed
2.Political equality
3.Accountability of the ruled to the ruler

Which of the above feature/features outline democratic rule?
en.explanation:
The correct choice is Option 1: 1 and 2 only.

Why?

Consent of the governed is fundamental to democracy.

Political equality (one person, one vote) is another core democratic principle.

Statement 3 (“accountability of the ruled to the ruler”) is the reverse of how democracy works—rulers are accountable to the people, not vice versa.

hi.content:
निम्नलिखित विशेषताओं पर विचार कीजिए:
1.शासितों की सहमति
2.राजनीतिक समानता
3.शासित लोगों की शासक के प्रति जवाबदेही
उपयुक्त में से कौन-सी विशेषता/विशेषताएं लोकतांत्रिक शासन को रेखांकित करती है/हैं?
hi.explanation:
सही विकल्प है विकल्प 1: केवल 1 और 2।

क्यों?

शासितों की सहमति लोकतंत्र की बुनियादी शर्त है।

राजनीतिक समानता (एक व्यक्ति, एक मत) लोकतंत्र का एक अन्य मूल सिद्धांत है।

कथन 3 ("शासितों की शासनकर्त्ता को उत्तरदायित्व") लोकतंत्र की कार्यप्रणाली का विपरीत है—लोकतंत्र में शासक जनता के प्रति उत्तरदायी होते हैं, न कि उसके विपरीत।

question:
Placing the earliest first, arrange the following countries in the chronological order in which they granted universal adult franchise :
1.USA
2.Sri Lanka
3.Japan
4.India
Select the answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Placing the earliest first, arrange the following countries in the chronological order in which they granted universal adult franchise :
1.USA
2.Sri Lanka
3.Japan
4.India
Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
The chronological order (earliest first) in which these countries enacted universal adult franchise is:

USA – 1920 (19th Amendment extended voting to women; 15th Amendment of 1870 had already enfranchised Black men in principle)

Sri Lanka – 1931 (Ceylon Constitution introduced full adult franchise)

Japan – 1945 (post-war reforms granted women the vote; first election under this was in 1946)

India – 1950 (Constitution came into force on 26 Jan 1950 with universal adult franchise)

So the correct sequence is 1, 2, 3, 4 (Option 1).

hi.content:
प्रारंभिक से पहले रखते हुए, निम्नलिखित देशों को उस कालानुक्रम में व्यवस्थित कीजिए, जिस क्रम में उन्होंने सार्वभौमिक वयस्क मताधिकार को स्वीकृति प्रदान की:
1.यूएसए (USA)
2.श्रीलंका
3.जापान
4.भारत
नीचे दिए गए कूट का प्रयोग कर उत्तर चुनिए:
hi.explanation:
इन देशों द्वारा सार्वभौम वयस्क मताधिकार लागू करने का कालानुक्रमिक क्रम (सबसे पहले लागू करने वाला पहले):

संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका – 1920 (19वें संशोधन द्वारा महिलाओं को मतदान का अधिकार मिला; 1870 के 15वें संशोधन द्वारा सैद्धांतिक रूप से अश्वेत पुरुषों को मताधिकार पहले ही मिल चुका था)

श्रीलंका – 1931 (सीलोन संविधान द्वारा पूर्ण वयस्क मताधिकार की शुरुआत)

जापान – 1945 (युद्धोपरांत सुधारों के तहत महिलाओं को मतदान का अधिकार मिला; इसके तहत पहला चुनाव 1946 में हुआ)

भारत – 1950 (संविधान 26 जनवरी 1950 को लागू हुआ, जिसमें सार्वभौम वयस्क मताधिकार का प्रावधान है)

इसलिए सही क्रम है 1, 2, 3, 4 (विकल्प 1)।

question:
Consider the following statements about the Linguistic Reorganization of the States:

1. Gandhiji, in 1948, had argued for the reorganization of provinces on a linguistic basis

2. The Constituent Assembly recommended it to be incorporated in the Constitution of India

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about the Linguistic Reorganization of the States:

1. Gandhiji, in 1948, had argued for the reorganization of provinces on a linguistic basis

2. The Constituent Assembly recommended it to be incorporated in the Constitution of India

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 4: Neither 1 nor 2.

Here’s a breakdown of why both statements are incorrect:

Statement 1: This is inaccurate. Gandhiji was assassinated in January 1948. While he and the Congress party had supported the idea of linguistic provinces during the freedom struggle, the mood changed dramatically after the partition of India in 1947. Leaders, including Gandhiji, became very concerned that creating states based on language could encourage regionalism and threaten the unity of the new nation. He prioritized national unity over linguistic reorganization at that time.

Statement 2: This is also incorrect. The Constituent Assembly did not recommend incorporating linguistic reorganization into the Constitution. In fact, it took steps to postpone the decision.

It appointed the Dhar Commission in 1948, which advised against creating states solely on a linguistic basis. The commission recommended that administrative convenience should be the primary factor.

Later, the JVP Committee (composed of Jawaharlal Nehru, Vallabhbhai Patel, and Pattabhi Sitaramayya) also reviewed the issue and concluded that the time was not right for creating new provinces based on language, as it could endanger national unity.

question:
Consider the following statements:

1. The duties of the Attorney General of India are to give advice to the Government of India upon such legal matters, and to perform such other duties of a legal character, as may from time to time be referred to or assigned to him by the President of India

2. The Solicitor General of India is the secondary Law Officer of the country, who assists the Attorney General, and is himself assisted by several Additional Solicitor Generals of India

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements:

1. The duties of the Attorney General of India are to give advice to the Government of India upon such legal matters, and to perform such other duties of a legal character, as may from time to time be referred to or assigned to him by the President of India

2. The Solicitor General of India is the secondary Law Officer of the country, who assists the Attorney General, and is himself assisted by several Additional Solicitor Generals of India

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
Both statements are correct:

Article 76 of the Constitution says the Attorney-General for India is the principal legal adviser to the Union and “shall perform such duties of legal character as may be assigned to him by the President.”

The Solicitor-General is the second law officer, assists the Attorney-General, and in turn is assisted by several Additional Solicitor-Generals.

Answer: Both 1 and 2 are correct.

hi.content:
निम्नलिखित कथनों पर विचार कीजिए:

1. भारत के महान्यायवादी के कर्तव्य भारत सरकार को ऐसे विधिक मामलों पर सलाह देना और ऐसे अन्य विधिक प्रकृति के कर्तव्यों का पालन करना है, जो समय-समय पर भारत के राष्ट्रपति द्वारा उसे संदर्भित या सौंपे जा सकते हैं।

2. भारत का सॉलिसिटर जनरल देश का द्वितीय विधि अधिकारी है, जो महान्यायवादी की सहायता करता है, और स्वयं भारत के कई अतिरिक्त सॉलिसिटर जनरलों द्वारा सहायता प्राप्त है।

ऊपर दिए गए कौन से कथन सही है/हैं?
hi.explanation:
दोनों कथन सही हैं:

संविधान का अनुच्छेद 76 कहता है कि भारत के महान्यायवादी केंद्र सरकार के मुख्य विधिक सलाहकार होते हैं और “राष्ट्रपति द्वारा उन्हें सौंपे गए ऐसे विधिक कार्य करेंगे, जो विधिक स्वभाव के हों।”

सॉलिसिटर जनरल दूसरा विधि अधिकारी होता है, जो महान्यायवादी की सहायता करता है, और उसकी सहायता के लिए कई अतिरिक्त सॉलिसिटर जनरल होते हैं।

उत्तर: दोनों 1 और 2 सही हैं।

2. Estimates Committee

3. Committee on Public Under- takings

en.content:
Which of the following Committees does NOT consist of Members from the Upper House of the Parliament?

1. Public Accounts Committee

2. Estimates Committee

3. Committee on Public Under- takings

Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
The only one of these three committees drawn exclusively from the Lok Sabha is the Estimates Committee. Both the Public Accounts Committee and the Committee on Public Undertakings include members of the Rajya Sabha.

Hence, the correct answer is:

Option 3: 2 only.

hi.content:

question:
Which of the following statements with regard to Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India is/are correct?

1. Freedoms under this Article are not absolute

2. Reasonable restrictions can be imposed on the exercise of freedoms under this Article

Select the answer using the code given below:

en.content:
Which of the following statements with regard to Article 19(1) of the Constitution of India is/are correct?

1. Freedoms under this Article are not absolute

2. Reasonable restrictions can be imposed on the exercise of freedoms under this Article

Select the answer using the code given below:
en.explanation:
Both statements are correct.

Article 19(1) freedoms are not absolute.

Parliament may impose “reasonable restrictions” on them (see Articles 19(2)–(6) for grounds like public order, security of the state, morality, etc.).

Answer: Option 3 (Both 1 and 2).

hi.content:
भारतीय संविधान के अनुच्छेद 19(1) के संबंध में निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा/से कथन सही है/हैं?

1. इस अनुच्छेद के अंतर्गत स्वतंत्रताएँ पूर्ण नहीं हैं

2. इस अनुच्छेद के अंतर्गत स्वतंत्रताओं के प्रयोग पर उचित प्रतिबंध लगाए जा सकते हैं

नीचे दिए गए कूट का प्रयोग करके उत्तर चुनिए:
hi.explanation:
दोनों कथन सही हैं।

अनुच्छेद 19(1) के अंतर्गत मिलने वाली स्वतंत्रताएँ निरपेक्ष (पूर्ण) नहीं हैं।

संसद इन पर "यथोचित प्रतिबंध" (reasonably restrictions) लगा सकती है (अनुच्छेद 19(2) से 19(6) देखें, जिनमें सार्वजनिक व्यवस्था, राज्य की सुरक्षा, नैतिकता आदि कारण शामिल हैं)।

उत्तर: विकल्प 3 (1 और 2 दोनों)।

en.content: "Not me, but you" is the motto of which one of the following youth organizations/schemes?
en.explanation:

en.content:
Which of the following statements about 'ULLAS' scheme is/are NOT correct?

1. It's a Centrally Sponsored scheme which aims at securing childhood for street children

2. It's a scheme aligned with National Education Policy-2020 which aims at empowering adults aged 15 or above who could not get due schooling

Select the answer using the code given below:
en.explanation:
"It's a Centrally Sponsored scheme which aims at securing childhood for street children"

The first part, that it's a Centrally Sponsored scheme, is actually correct!

However, the second part about "aiming at securing childhood for street children" is incorrect. ULLAS, which stands for "Understanding LifeLong Learning for All in Society" (and is essentially the new name for the "New India Literacy Programme"), is all about adult education and literacy, not specifically about street children. Schemes for street children usually fall under child protection and welfare programs. So, this statement is NOT correct.

"It's a scheme aligned with National Education Policy-2020 which aims at empowering adults aged 15 or above who could not get due schooling"

This statement is spot on! ULLAS is indeed aligned with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.

Its primary goal is precisely to empower adults aged 15 years and above who missed out on formal schooling, providing them with basic literacy and lifelong learning opportunities. So, this statement is correct.

Since the question asks for the statement(s) that are NOT correct, only statement 1 fits that description.

Therefore, the answer is:

1 only

पहला भाग, कि यह एक केंद्रीय प्रायोजित योजना है, वास्तव में सही है!

"यह राष्ट्रीय शिक्षा नीति, 2020 के अनुरूप एक योजना है जिसका उद्देश्य उन 15 वर्ष या उससे अधिक आयु के वयस्कों को सशक्त बनाना है जिन्हें समुचित स्कूली शिक्षा नहीं मिल सकी"

यह कथन बिल्कुल सही है! ULLAS वास्तव में राष्ट्रीय शिक्षा नीति, 2020 के अनुरूप है।

इसका प्राथमिक लक्ष्य औपचारिक स्कूली शिक्षा से वंचित 15 वर्ष या उससे अधिक आयु के वयस्कों को मूलभूत साक्षरता और आजीवन अधिगम के अवसर प्रदान कर उन्हें सशक्त बनाना है। इसलिए, यह कथन सही है।

question: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct about NATO ?

en.content: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct about NATO ?

options.content: It was formed out of the signing of the Washington Treaty
Russia is not a member of this organization
It derives its authority from the UN Charter

en.explanation:
Let's break down each statement about NATO to figure out which one isn't quite right!

Option 1: It was formed out of the signing of the Washington Treaty.

This is absolutely correct! NATO came into being with the signing of the North Atlantic Treaty, also known as the Washington Treaty, on April 4, 1949. This treaty laid out the foundation for the alliance and its principles.

Option 2: Russia is not a member of this organization.

This is also correct. Russia has never been a member of NATO. In fact, NATO was originally formed as a counterweight to the Soviet Union (of which Russia was the largest part) during the Cold War.

Option 3: It derives its authority from the UN Charter.

This statement is NOT correct. While NATO's operations, especially those related to collective self-defense (like Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty), are consistent with Article 51 of the UN Charter, which recognizes the inherent right of individual or collective self-defense, NATO does not "derive its authority" directly from the UN Charter. NATO is an independent international organization established by its member states through the North Atlantic Treaty. Its authority comes from that treaty, not from the UN.

Option 4: Finland is its latest member.

This is correct! Finland joined NATO on April 4, 2023, becoming the 31st member of the alliance.

So, the statement that is NOT correct about NATO is Option 3.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कथन नाटो के बारे में सही नहीं है?

options.content: यह वाशिंगटन संधि पर हस्ताक्षर करने से बना था।
रूस इस संगठन का सदस्य नहीं है।
यह अपना अधिकार संयुक्त राष्ट्र चार्टर से प्राप्त करता है।

hi.explanation:
आइए NATO के बारे में प्रत्येक कथन का विश्लेषण करें ताकि यह पता चल सके कि कौन सा कथन बिल्कुल सही नहीं है!

विकल्प 1: यह वाशिंगटन संधि के हस्ताक्षर से गठित हुआ था।

यह बिल्कुल सही है! NATO की स्थापना 4 अप्रैल, 1949 को उत्तरी अटलांटिक संधि (जिसे वाशिंगटन संधि भी कहा जाता है) के हस्ताक्षर के साथ हुई थी। इस संधि में संगठन की आधारशिला और इसके सिद्धांत निर्धारित किए गए थे।

विकल्प 2: रूस इस संगठन का सदस्य नहीं है।

यह भी सही है। रूस कभी भी NATO का सदस्य नहीं रहा। वास्तव में, शीत युद्ध के दौरान NATO की स्थापना सोवियत संघ (जिसका सबसे बड़ा भाग रूस था) का प्रतिकार करने के लिए की गई थी।

विकल्प 3: इसका अधिकार, संयुक्त राष्ट्र चार्टर से प्राप्त होता है।

यह कथन सही नहीं है। भले ही NATO की कार्रवाइयाँ, विशेष रूप से सामूहिक आत्म-सुरक्षा से सम्बंधित कार्रवाइयाँ (जैसे कि उत्तरी अटलांटिक संधि का अनुच्छेद 5), संयुक्त राष्ट्र चार्टर के अनुच्छेद 51 के अनुरूप हैं, जो व्यक्तिगत या सामूहिक आत्म-सुरक्षा के अधिकार को स्वीकार करता है, लेकिन NATO का अधिकार सीधे संयुक्त राष्ट्र चार्टर से नहीं आता है। NATO एक स्वतंत्र अंतरराष्ट्रीय संगठन है जिसकी स्थापना उसके सदस्य राष्ट्रों द्वारा उत्तरी अटलांटिक संधि के माध्यम से की गई थी। इसका अधिकार उस संधि से प्राप्त होता है, न कि संयुक्त राष्ट्र से।

विकल्प 4: फिनलैंड इसका नवीनतम सदस्य है।

यह सही है! फिनलैंड 4 अप्रैल, 2023 को NATO में शामिल हुआ, जिसके बाद यह गठबंधन का 31वां सदस्य बना।

इस प्रकार, NATO के बारे में जो कथन सही नहीं है, वह है विकल्प 3।

1. Religious or social practices of Nagas

2. Naga customary law and procedure

3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources

4. Boundaries of Nagaland with other states

Select the answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Under Article 371A of the Constitution of India, with respect to which of the following item(s) the Acts of the Parliament of India shall not apply to the State of Nagaland, unless the Legislative Assembly of the State of Nagaland by a resolution so decides ?

1. Religious or social practices of Nagas

2. Naga customary law and procedure

3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources

4. Boundaries of Nagaland with other states

Select the answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
Article 371A of the Constitution of India provides special provisions with respect to the State of Nagaland. According to this article, certain Acts of the Parliament of India shall not apply to the State of Nagaland unless the Legislative Assembly of the State of Nagaland decides by a resolution that they should. The specified items include:

1. Religious or social practices of Nagas

2. Naga customary law and procedure

3. Ownership and transfer of land and its resources

There is no mention of the boundaries of Nagaland with other states under Article 371A.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: 1, 2, and 3 only

question:
Under Article 191 of the Constitution of India, a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state if the person holds any office of profit under :

1. the Government of India.

2. any State Government.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :

en.content:
Under Article 191 of the Constitution of India, a person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state if the person holds any office of profit under :

1. the Government of India.

2. any State Government.

Select the correct answer using the code given below :
en.explanation:
Article 191 of the Constitution of India outlines the grounds for disqualification for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a state. One of the conditions specified is holding an office of profit. This clause is aimed at ensuring that the legislator does not have a conflict of interest due to their financial ties with the government.

According to Article 191 (1)(a) :

"A person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a State if he holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State specified in the First Schedule, other than an office declared by the Legislature of the State by law not to disqualify its holder."

Thus, the person shall be disqualified for holding any office of profit under :

1. The Government of India.

2. Any State Government.

Given this explanation, the correct answer is :

Option C : Both 1 and 2

1. भारत सरकार के अधीन कोई लाभ का पद धारण करता है ।

2. किसी राज्य सरकार के अधीन कोई लाभ का पद धारण करता है ।

"A person shall be disqualified for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly or Legislative Council of a State if he holds any office of profit under the Government of India or the Government of any State specified in the First Schedule, other than an office declared by the Legislature of the State by law not to disqualify its holder."

Thus, the person shall be disqualified for holding any office of profit under :

1. The Government of India.

2. Any State Government.

Given this explanation, the correct answer is :

Option C : Both 1 and 2

Statement 2 : Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 263 of the Constitution of India.

This statement is also incorrect. The Finance Commission is constituted in pursuance to Article 280 of the Constitution of India, not Article 263. Article 280 provides for the establishment of a Finance Commission by the President to recommend the distribution of financial resources between the Union and the States.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Neither 1 nor 2

question: Which one of the following provisions of the Constitution of India deals with District Planning Committee?

en.content: Which one of the following provisions of the Constitution of India deals with District Planning Committee?
en.explanation:
To identify which provision of the Constitution of India deals with the District Planning Committee, we need to refer to Part IXA which focuses on "The Municipalities." The specific provisions related to District Planning Committees are found under this part.

Article 243ZD of the Constitution of India mandates the establishment of District Planning Committees at the district level to consolidate the plans prepared by Panchayats and the Municipalities in the district. It aims to create a comprehensive plan for the entire district by considering local level plans and providing a platform for decentralized planning.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A - 243ZD

Below is the detailed explanation of Article 243ZD:

Article 243ZD states:

"(1) There shall be constituted in every State at the district level a District Planning Committee to consolidate the plans prepared by the Panchayats and the Municipalities in the district and to prepare a draft development plan for the district as a whole.

(2) The Legislature of a State may, by law, make provision with respect to—

(a) the composition of the District Planning Committees;

(b) the manner in which the seats in such Committees shall be filled:

(c) the functions relating to district planning which may be assigned to such Committees;

(d) the manner in which the Chairpersons of such Committees shall be chosen."

question:
Consider the following statements about Attorney-General in India:

1. She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India

2. She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote

3. She/he is appointed by the President of India

4. She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law

How many of the statements given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements about Attorney-General in India:

1. She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India

2. She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote

3. She/he is appointed by the President of India

4. She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law

How many of the statements given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
To determine how many of the statements about the Attorney-General in India are correct, let's examine each statement individually:

Statement 1: She/he is the first law officer of the Government of India

This is correct. The Attorney-General is indeed the first law officer of the Government of India, providing legal advice to the Government on various legal matters.

Statement 2: She/he has a right to speak in any House of Parliament without any right to vote

This is also correct. The Attorney-General has the right to speak in both the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha, as well as their joint sittings, but does not have the right to vote.

Statement 3: She/he is appointed by the President of India

This is correct. The Attorney-General is appointed by the President of India on the advice of the Government.

Statement 4: She/he is a whole-time counsel for the Government in the court of law

This is incorrect. The Attorney-General is not necessarily a whole-time counsel for the Government. He/she can also engage in private practice, provided it does not conflict with his/her duties to the government.

Therefore, the correct statements are 1, 2, and 3, making a total of three correct statements.

Correct Option: Option C: 3

question: By which one of the following amendments, was Article 51A, relating to the Fundamental Duties, inserted into the Constitution of India?

en.content: By which one of the following amendments, was Article 51A, relating to the Fundamental Duties, inserted into the Constitution of India?
en.explanation:
The correct answer is The Constitution (42nd Amendment) Act.

Fundamental Duties:

  • The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, enacted in 1976, added Article 51A to the Indian Constitution, incorporating Fundamental Duties.

  • These duties were inspired by the Constitution of the former USSR.

  • The amendment followed the recommendations of the Swaran Singh Committee.

  • Initially, 10 Fundamental Duties were added under a new section, Part IVA.

  • In 2002, the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act added an 11th duty, emphasizing the provision of educational opportunities for children aged 6 to 14 years.

  • These duties apply exclusively to Indian citizens and are not enforceable by law.

85th Amendment Act, 2001:

  • This amendment introduced 'consequential seniority' for promotions of Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe government employees, effective retrospectively from June 1995.

92nd Amendment Act, 2003:

  • It included four more languages in the Eighth Schedule: Bodo, Dogri (Dongri), Maithili, and Santhali, increasing the total number of constitutionally recognized languages to 22.

44th Amendment Act, 1978:

  • It replaced 'internal disturbance' with 'armed rebellion' in the context of a national emergency.

  • The President was required to declare a national emergency based on the written recommendation of the Cabinet.

  • It introduced procedural safeguards regarding national emergency and the President's rule.

  • The right to property was reclassified from a fundamental right to a mere legal right.

  • It ensured that the fundamental rights guaranteed by Articles 20 and 21 could not be suspended during a national emergency.

question: Which one of the following is not a power of Panchayats under Article 243G?

en.content: Which one of the following is not a power of Panchayats under Article 243G?
en.explanation:
Under Article 243G of the Indian Constitution, the Panchayats are granted the authority to plan and implement schemes for economic development and social justice. The powers and responsibilities are extensive and cover several areas related to land management and improvements.

Let's break down the options to identify which one is not a power of Panchayats under Article 243G:

  • Option A: Land improvement

    • This is typically within the purview of Panchayats. They are involved in activities aimed at improving land productivity and utility.

  • Option B: Implementation of land reforms

    • Panchayats may also play a role in implementing land reforms as part of their duty to ensure equitable distribution and efficient use of land resources.

  • Option C: Land consolidation and soil conservation

    • These are typically considered responsibilities of Panchayats, focusing on optimizing land use and improving soil health.

  • Option D: Regulation of land revenue

    • This function usually falls under the jurisdiction of state governments rather than Panchayats. The regulation and collection of land revenue involve complex legal and administrative processes typically handled by state-level authorities.

Therefore, Option D: Regulation of land revenue is not a power of Panchayats under Article 243G.

question: With regard to the Constitution of India, which of the following statements is not correct?

en.content: With regard to the Constitution of India, which of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: The words - Socialist and Secular, were not originally part of the Constitution
The Preamble states the objects of the Constitution of India
The Preamble is enforceable in a Court of Law
A Republic refers to the people as the source of all authority under the Constitution

en.explanation:
Option C: The Preamble is enforceable in a Court of Law

The Preamble to the Constitution of India serves as an introduction and sets out the guiding principles and objectives of the Constitution. However, it is not considered enforceable by a court since it does not confer any legal rights or obligations. Instead, it provides the framework and philosophy that guide the interpretation of the Constitution's provisions. Therefore, the statement that the Preamble is enforceable in a court of law is not correct.

hi.content: भारतीय संविधान के संबंध में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

प्रस्तावना भारत के संविधान के उद्देश्यों को बताती है।
एक गणतंत्र लोगों को संविधान के तहत सभी प्राधिकरणों के स्रोत के रूप में संदर्भित करता है।

options: Speaker shall preside over the House of the People.
Speaker will cast vote in the first instance in the House.
Speaker will have the power to maintain order within the House of the People.
Speaker can adjourn the House or suspend the meeting till there is a quorum.
question: Which one of the following is not a power of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?

en.content: Which one of the following is not a power of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha?

options.content: Speaker shall preside over the House of the People.
Speaker will cast vote in the first instance in the House.
Speaker will have the power to maintain order within the House of the People.
Speaker can adjourn the House or suspend the meeting till there is a quorum.

en.explanation:
The statement that is not a power of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is Option B: Speaker will cast vote in the first instance in the House.

While the Speaker does have a casting vote in case of a tie, it's not their first instance vote. The Speaker typically only votes in the event of a tie, after all other members have cast their votes. Let's clarify the other options:

Option A: Speaker shall preside over the House of the People. This is a core function of the Speaker. They chair the sessions of the Lok Sabha, ensuring the smooth conduct of business.

Option C: Speaker will have the power to maintain order within the House of the People. The Speaker is responsible for upholding decorum and order within the proceedings. They have the authority to reprimand members, suspend sittings, or even expel disruptive members as per the rules of procedure.

Option D: Speaker can adjourn the House or suspend the meeting till there is a quorum. A quorum is the minimum number of members required to conduct business. The Speaker has the authority to adjourn the House or suspend the sitting if the required quorum isn't present. This ensures that decisions made are representative of a sufficient number of elected members.

In essence, the Speaker's role is crucial for the smooth and effective functioning of the Lok Sabha, encompassing presiding over the sessions, maintaining order, and ensuring that parliamentary procedures are followed correctly. The casting vote is a power, but it's contingent upon a tie, not their initial vote.

अध्यक्ष सदन को स्थगित कर सकता है या गणपूर्ती पूरा होने तक बैठक को स्थगित कर सकता है।

hi.explanation:
लोक सभा के अध्यक्ष की शक्तियों में निम्नलिखित शामिल होते हैं:

अध्यक्ष लोक सभा की अध्यक्षता करता है।

अध्यक्ष के पास सदन के भीतर व्यवस्था बनाए रखने की शक्ति होती है।

अध्यक्ष सदन को स्थगित कर सकता है या गणपूर्ति न होने पर बैठक को स्थगित कर सकता है।

विकल्प B, "अध्यक्ष सदन में पहली बार मत डालेंगे," सही नहीं है क्योंकि अध्यक्ष आमतौर पर तटस्थ रहता है और केवल उत्पन्न स्थिति के कारण वोट डालता है जब सदन में मत टाई हो जाए।

इसलिए, विकल्प B अध्यक्ष की शक्ति नहीं है।

question: Which one of the following is included in Article 51A (Part-IV A) of the Constitution of India?

en.content: Which one of the following is included in Article 51A (Part-IV A) of the Constitution of India?
en.explanation:
Article 51A of the Constitution of India is included in Part IV-A and is specifically related to Fundamental Duties. This article was added to the Constitution by the 42nd Amendment in 1976. It outlines the moral obligations of all citizens to help promote a spirit of patriotism and uphold the unity of India. The Fundamental Duties are not enforceable by law but are considered important for the moral and ethical development of citizens.

Fundamental Duties under Article 51A include:

Abide by the Constitution: To respect its ideals and institutions, the National Flag, and the National Anthem.

Cherish the Noble Ideals: Those which inspired the national struggle for freedom.

Uphold and Protect the Sovereignty: Ensure the unity and integrity of India.

Defend the Country: When called upon, to render national service.

Promote Harmony: Promote the spirit of common brotherhood among all people of India transcending religious, linguistic, and regional diversities.

Value and Preserve the Rich Heritage: Of India’s composite culture.

Protect the Natural Environment: Including forests, lakes, rivers, and wildlife.

Develop Scientific Temper: Cultivate humanism and the spirit of inquiry and reform.

Safeguard Public Property: To abjure violence.

Strive Towards Excellence: In all spheres of individual and collective activity.

Duty of Parents: To provide opportunities for education to their child or ward between the ages of six and fourteen years.

These duties serve as a constant reminder to the citizens to build a disciplined and responsible society.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन भारत के संविधान के अनुच्छेद 51A (भाग- IV A) में शामिल है?
hi.explanation: भारत के संविधान के अनुच्छेद 51A (भाग- IV A) में "मौलिक कर्तव्य" शामिल हैं। भाग IV A, जिसे संविधान (बयालीसवां संशोधन) अधिनियम, 1976 के माध्यम से जोड़ा गया था, भारतीय नागरिकों के लिए 11 मौलिक कर्तव्यों का उल्लेख करता है। इन कर्तव्यों में संविधान का पालन करना, राष्ट्र की स्वतंत्रता के लिए संघर्ष के आदर्शों का सम्मान करना, भारत की संप्रभुता, एकता और अखंडता की रक्षा करना, राष्ट्र की सेवा करना, ध्वज और राष्ट्रगान का सम्मान करना, और पर्यावरण संरक्षण आदि शामिल हैं।

Article 15 specifically prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth. It ensures that the state cannot discriminate against any citizen, including women, and provides for the complete equality of men and women.

The other options are incorrect:

Option B: Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India deals with provisions related to the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes.

Option C: The Indian Independence Act was a legislation passed by the British Parliament in 1947, which led to the partition of India and the creation of Pakistan. It does not address gender equality.

Option D: Article 20 provides protection against conviction for offenses, including double jeopardy and self-incrimination, but it does not deal with gender equality.

Thus, Articles 14 and 15 are the constitutional provisions that ensure the complete equality of men and women in India.

question:
Which of the following statements about the non-permanent members of the Security Council of the United Nations is/are correct?

1. Their total number is now 10, but was originally only 6.

2. They are elected for a term of two years only.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:

en.content:
Which of the following statements about the non-permanent members of the Security Council of the United Nations is/are correct?

1. Their total number is now 10, but was originally only 6.

2. They are elected for a term of two years only.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
en.explanation:
Option C: Both 1 and 2

The total number of non-permanent members of the United Nations Security Council is currently 10. Originally, when the United Nations was founded in 1945, there were only 6 non-permanent members. This number was increased to 10 in 1965 as part of a reform to make the council more representative.

Non-permanent members of the Security Council are elected for a term of two years. These members are chosen by the General Assembly, and each year, five of the non-permanent seats are up for election, ensuring that the council has a steady rotation of countries without the possibility of immediate re-election, thus encouraging broad geographical representation.

question: Fundamental Rights guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is available only to the citizens of India?

en.content: Fundamental Rights guaranteed under which one of the following Articles of the Constitution of India is available only to the citizens of India?
en.explanation:
Under Article-19 of the Constitution of India, Fundamental Rights are guaranteed only to the citizens of India. There are five fundamental articles which are only to Indian citizens i.e. Articles-15, 16, 19, 29, 30. This article guarantees the six rights. These are :

1. Right to freedom of speech and expression.

2. Right to assemble peacefully and without arms.

3. Right to form associations or unions or co-operations.

4. Right to move freely throughout the territory of India.

5. Right to reside and settle in any part of the territory of India.

6. Right to practice any profession or to carry on any occupation, trade or business.

While Article 20 deals with the protection of certain rights incase of conviction for offences. Article 21 deals with the protection of life and persons liberty, and Article 22 grants protection to persons who are arrested or detained.

question: Consider the following description of the Samadhi of a former Prime Minister of India. Central Samadhi Platform comprises nine square black polished granite solid stone blocks, capped with a 'Diya' in the centre. The number nine holds significance and represents the Navarasas, Navaratras and Navagrahas. Then placement of the Nine-Square Samadhi is in a circular lotus shaped pattern. The Nine-Square platform is accessed in four cardinal directions by pathways made in white composite tiles so that the floor does not get heated. Identify the Samadhi.

en.content: Consider the following description of the Samadhi of a former Prime Minister of India. Central Samadhi Platform comprises nine square black polished granite solid stone blocks, capped with a 'Diya' in the centre. The number nine holds significance and represents the Navarasas, Navaratras and Navagrahas. Then placement of the Nine-Square Samadhi is in a circular lotus shaped pattern. The Nine-Square platform is accessed in four cardinal directions by pathways made in white composite tiles so that the floor does not get heated. Identify the Samadhi.
en.explanation:
The Samadhi, which is mentioned in above question, is Sadaiv Atal. It is the crematorium (Samadhi) of former Prime Minister of India, Atal Bihari Vajpayee (1924-2018). He served three terms as the Prime Minister of India. He was the first Indian Prime Minister who was not a member of the Indian National Congress party to have served a full five year term in office.

Shakti Sthal is a memorial of former Prime Minister Indira Gandhi.

Shantivan is the Samadhi or Cremation spot of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and the Veer Bhumi is a memorial dedicated to the former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi.

en.content: Which member of the Constituent Assembly proposed the resolution that the National Flag of India be a "horizontal tricolour of saffron, white and dark green in equal proportion", with a wheel in navy blue at the centre?
en.explanation: In 1947, when India gained freedom from the British, a committee headed by Dr. Rajendra Prasad, decided to adopt the flag of the Congress as the National Flag of India with a few modifications. With this in mind, the flag of 1931 was adopted as the National Flag of India, but the Charkha in the middle was replaced with the Ashoka Chakra. In National Flag the saffron colour on top represents sacrifice, white represents peace and purity and green signifies the Law of Dharma (righteousness).

en.explanation:
Fundamental Rights are the basic rights of the common people and inalienable rights of the people who enjoy it under the charter of rights contained in Part III (Article-12 to 35) of the Constitution of India. Right to Citizenship is not a Fundamental Right. All the Fundamental Rights have been classified under the following six categories.

1. Right to be Equality (Articles 14-18)

2. Right to Freedom (Articles 19-22)

3. Right against Exploitation (Articles 23-24)

4. Right to Freedom of Religion (Articles 25-28)

5. Cultural and Educational Rights (Articles 29-30)

6. Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32)

The Right to Property is no longer a Fundamental Right.

options.content: Equal justice and free legal aid.
Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance.

en.explanation:
Part-IV of Indian Constitution deals with Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP). Basic aim of DPSP is to set-up social and economic goals before the law makers. Some of the important DPSPs are

$$\bullet$$ Uniform civil code for the citizens.

$$\bullet$$ Provide free and compulsory education for children below 14 years.

$$\bullet$$ To promote international peace and amity.

$$\bullet$$ Protection of personal law does not come under Directive Principles of state policy.

$$\bullet$$ Protection and improvement of environment and safeguarding of forests and wildlife.

$$\bullet$$ Organization of Village Panchayats and to promote cottage industry.

$$\bullet$$ Equal pay for equal work.

$$\bullet$$ Equal justice and free legal aid.

$$\bullet$$ Protection of monuments and places and objects of national importance.

$$\bullet$$ Separation of judiciary from executive.

Hence, option (c) is not a part of Directive Principles of State Policy.

question: The Cabinet Mission Plan for India envisaged a

en.content: The Cabinet Mission Plan for India envisaged a
en.explanation: The Cabinet Mission Plan for India envisaged a Union of States. The mission spent some three weeks to discuss with the leaders of various political parties, but could not arrive at any agreed solution. So, finally it announced its own recommendations on 16th May, 1946. The Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946 proposed that there shall be a Union of India which was to be empowered to deal with the defense, foreign affairs and communications. The Cabinet Mission recommended an undivided India and turned down the Muslim League's demand for a separate Pakistan. The Cabinet Mission restricted the Communal representation.

en.content: Which one among the following Acts for first time allowed Indians, at theoretically, entry to higher posts in British Indian administration?
en.explanation:
Charter Act, 1833 for first time allowed Indians, at theoretically, entry to higher posts in British Indian Administration. The Charter Act of 1833 was the first act which made provision to freely admit the natives of India to share administration in the country. This act also provided that Haileybury College of London should make quota to admit future civil servants.

Charter Act of 1813 compelled the East India Company to accept responsibility for the education of the Indian people.

Charles Wood's Education Despatch, 1854 promoted the use of vernacular languages in the primary schools.

Indian Charter Act, 1861 was passed by British Parliament for executive and legislative purposes.

en.content:
Consider the following statements.

1. The Constitution of India empowers the Parliament to form new states and to alter the areas, boundaries or names of existing states by passing a resolution in simple majority.

2. Jammu and Kashmir has been given special status under Article-370 of the Constitution of India.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?

question:
Consider the following statements.

1. The Amendment procedure has been provided in Article-368 of the Constitution of India.

2. The consent of the States is mandatory for all Amendments to the Constitution of India.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statements.

1. The Amendment procedure has been provided in Article-368 of the Constitution of India.

2. The consent of the States is mandatory for all Amendments to the Constitution of India.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
Article-368 of the Indian Constitution lays down the procedure for Amendment of the Constitution. This article provides for two types of amendments.

(i) By a special majority of Parliament.

(ii) Through ratification of half of the states by a simple majority.

Besides, some other articles also provide for Constitutional Amendment by simple majority of Parliament and no consent of states are required.

Hence, statement (1) is correct and (2) is incorrect.

question:
Consider the following statement(s) about the Constitution of India.

1. A Member of Parliament enjoys freedom of speech in the Parliament as a Parliamentary privilege protected by the Constitution of India.

2. The Constitution has vested the power to amend the Constitution in the Parliament.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?

en.content:
Consider the following statement(s) about the Constitution of India.

1. A Member of Parliament enjoys freedom of speech in the Parliament as a Parliamentary privilege protected by the Constitution of India.

2. The Constitution has vested the power to amend the Constitution in the Parliament.

Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?
en.explanation:
Indian Constitution provides parliamentary privileges to the members of the Parliament. These are special rights, immunities, exceptions enjoyed by the members of the two houses of Parliament e.g. A member of Parliament enjoys freedom of speech in the Parliament and no member is liable to any proceedings in any given court for anything said by him in Parliament. Article 368 of the Indian Constitution gives power to the Parliament to amend the Constitution.

Hence, both Statements are correct.

question: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is correct?

en.content: Which one of the following statements about Money Bill is correct?

options.content: A Bill shall be deemed to be a Money Bill only if it provides for imposition of fines or penalties.
A Money Bill shall be introduced in the Rajya Sabha.
The Rajya Sabha can reject the Money Bill.
The Speaker of the Lok Sabha finally decides if it is a Money Bill, should any dispute about it arise.

en.explanation:
Under Article 110(1) of the Constitution, a bill is deemed to be a Money Bill if it contains provisions dealing with following mattes namely

$$\bullet$$ Imposing, abolishing and regulating a tax

$$\bullet$$ Regulating government borrowings

$$\bullet$$ The consolidated and contingency funds of India etc.

Money Bills can be introduced only in Lok Sabha. Rajya Sabha cannot make amendments in it. Money Bill passed by Lok Sabha and transmitted to it. Speaker of the Lok Sabha finally decides if it is a Money Bill, should any disputes about it arise.

Hence, option (a), (b), (c) are incorrect and (d) is correct.

question: Which one of the following powers of the Prime Minister of India is codified in the Constitution of India itself?

en.content: Which one of the following powers of the Prime Minister of India is codified in the Constitution of India itself?

options.content: The power of advising the President as regards the appointment of other Ministers.
The power to allocate business amongst the Ministers.
The power to summon the meeting of the Cabinet.
The power to transfer a Minister from one department to another.

en.explanation:
Powers of Prime Minister are as follow

$$\bullet$$ He has the power of selecting other Ministers and also of advising the President to dismiss anyone of them individually or require anyone of them to resign. Virtually, the Ministers hold office at the pleasure of the Prime Minister.

$$\bullet$$ The Prime Minister takes decisions with regard to assignment of various ministers to individual ministers. He may transfer a Minister from one department to another.

$$\bullet$$ The Prime Minister stands between the President and the Cabinet. Communications relating to policy are made only through the Prime Minister.

Hence, option (a) is correct.

question: The two provisions of the Constitution of India that most clearly express the power of judicial review are

en.content: The two provisions of the Constitution of India that most clearly express the power of judicial review are
en.explanation:
Though the phrase 'Judicial Review' has nowhere been mentioned in the Indian Constitution, but Article-32 and Article-226 explicitly confers the power of judicial review on the Supreme Court and High Court, respectively. Under Article 32, the Supreme Court has power to issue directions or writs for the enforcement of any of the Fundamental Rights.

Under Article-226, the Constitutional validity of a Legislative enactment or an executive order can be challenge in a High Court on the grounds that it infringes the Fundamental Rights or it is outside the competence of the authority which framed it.

Under Article 352, the President can declare a national emergency when the security of India or a part of it is threatened by war or external aggression or armed rebellion.

en.explanation: The 'Basic Structure Doctrine' is a judiciary-made doctrine enunciated by the Supreme Court on 24th April, 1973 in Keshavananda Bharati case to put limitation on the amending powers of the Parliament.
